originalTitle: Fears for press freedom as billionaire takes control of East Africa's largest media house
title: Big News About News! A Story from Africa
content:
Hi, amazing friends across Africa! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter here. I have a really important story for you today. It’s about how we get our news, and why telling the truth is super-duper important! Sometimes, grown-ups talk about big things that happen in our world. Today's story is one of those big things. It's about a very large company that makes news for many countries in East Africa.

What is "News" Anyway?

News helps us learn about things happening all around us. It tells us about our towns, our countries, and even other places in Africa and the world! News can be about happy things, like a new school opening or a local football team winning. It can be about important things, like what our leaders are doing or a new way to help our farms grow food. News helps us understand our world better, so we can all be good citizens. We can get news from many places. Some people read newspapers with big, colorful pictures. Some people listen to the radio while they are working or traveling. Lots of us watch TV news in our homes. Many grown-ups also look at news on their phones or computers. All these places help us stay informed about what’s happening in our world every single day. *Did you know?* The very first newspapers in Africa started a very long time ago! They helped people share important messages and stories across different communities.

Meet the "News Houses"!

Imagine a big house, but instead of people living in it, it's where news is made! We call these places 'media houses' or 'news houses'. They are busy places with many clever people who work there. There are brave reporters who go out and find stories. They talk to people, visit different places, and ask lots of questions. There are photographers who take amazing pictures to show us what's happening. Then there are editors who make sure the stories are clear, correct, and easy for everyone to understand. They work very hard to make sure the news is ready for you to hear or read. East Africa has a very big news house. It's like the biggest news house in many countries nearby! It tells stories to millions and millions of people every day. It's a very important place because it helps so many people know what's going on.

A New Owner for the Big News House

Now, here's the big news we are talking about today! A very, very rich man bought this big news house. His name is Rostam Aziz. He has a lot of money, more than you can imagine! When someone buys a company, they become the owner. This means they get to make many big decisions about the company. They can decide how things are run and what kind of stories are told. Think of it like this: if you have a favorite toy, and someone buys the company that makes that toy, that new owner will decide what new toys to make or how the old toys will look. It's kind of the same for a news house. *Did you know?* Being a 'billionaire' means you have so much money, it's hard to count! It's enough money to build many schools or hospitals for communities.

Why is Telling the Truth So Important?

This is the most important part of our story, friends! News reporters have a super-important job. Their job is to find out the truth and share it with everyone. They try their very best to tell stories exactly as they happened, without changing anything. Why is the truth so important? Because when we know the truth, we can make good choices. If the news tells us about a new road being built, we know we might need to take a different way to school or to the market. If the news tells us about a big flood or a drought, we know to be careful and help our neighbors. Knowing the truth helps us stay safe and smart. If the news doesn't tell us the whole truth, or tells us something that isn't true, then we might make wrong choices. That's not good for anyone, right? We need to trust what we hear and read. *Did you know?* Reporters in Africa work very hard! They travel to many places, sometimes far away in the bush or busy cities, to find amazing stories for you.

What Does "Being Independent" Mean?

Imagine you are playing a game with your friends. You want to play fairly, right? You want to make your own choices about how to play, without someone telling you to cheat or to let them win. That's a bit like 'being independent'! For news reporters, 'being independent' means they can tell stories freely. No one should tell them: 'Don't talk about that person!' or 'Only say good things about this project!' They should be free to report *all* the important facts, even if those facts are about someone powerful or something that's not perfect. When reporters are independent, it means they are working for *you*, the people who watch and read the news. They are not working for the owner of the news house or for a rich person who wants to control what is said.

Why Are People Asking Questions?

Now that a very rich person, Rostam Aziz, owns this big news house, some people are asking questions. They are wondering if the news will still be able to tell *all* the stories freely. They hope that the reporters will still be independent and only tell the truth, no matter what. It's like when a new teacher comes to your class. You hope the new teacher will still teach you all the fun and important things, and be fair to everyone. People are hoping the news house will still be fair and tell the true stories, without anyone telling them to change things. This is very important for something called 'press freedom'. 'Press freedom' means that news companies and reporters should be free to do their job without fear. They should be free to report the truth, even about powerful people, without getting into trouble for it. It's important that many voices can be heard, not just the voice of one person. This helps everyone in our countries in East Africa to know what is happening and to have different ideas and opinions. *Did you know?* Many countries in Africa celebrate 'Freedom Day'. It's a special day to remember how important it is for everyone to be free to think, speak, and share ideas.

Why Does This Matter to You?

You might be a kid, but this story matters to you too! When you grow up, you will rely on the news to help you understand your world, make choices, and even vote for leaders who can make your country better. We all want to live in a place where we know what's really happening. We want to know that the news we hear or read is fair and true. This helps us learn, grow, and build better families and communities in Africa. So, when you see news on TV or hear it on the radio, remember that someone worked hard to find that story. And remember how important it is that they could tell you the truth, freely and fairly!

Let's Keep Being Curious!

So, what can you do? You can keep being curious! Ask questions about what you hear and see. Talk to your grown-ups about the news. And always remember that finding out the truth helps all of us. Let's hope that the big news house in East Africa keeps telling all the amazing and important stories, truly and freely, for everyone in our wonderful continent!
summary: A very rich man named Rostam Aziz bought a big news company in East Africa, making people wonder if the news will still be free to tell the truth to everyone.
whyMatters: It's important because we all need to hear true and fair stories from news reporters to understand our world, learn new things, and make good choices for ourselves and our communities.

originalTitle: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
title: A Special Drum Comes Home to Africa!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hi everyone! I'm your friendly news reporter. I have some super exciting news to share with you today! It's a happy story about something very special coming home to Africa. It's a big celebration!

A Talking Drum Returns!

Imagine a drum that can talk! Well, not really with words, but with amazing sounds. We are talking about a very special 'talking drum'. This drum has traveled a long, long way. Now, it is finally back where it belongs! This makes many people very happy.

Where Did It Go?

This talking drum belongs to a country in Africa called Ivory Coast. Can you say that? Ivory Coast! It's a beautiful country with wonderful people. A very long time ago, this special drum was taken from Ivory Coast. It went to a faraway country called France. France is in Europe, very far from Africa.

Why Was It in France?

Many, many years ago, there was something called 'colonial rule'. This means people from faraway countries came to live in some parts of Africa. They sometimes took special things from these African countries. These things were taken to their own countries. They stayed there for a very long time. The talking drum was one of those special things. It was taken many years ago. It has been in a museum in France for a very, very long time.

What is a Talking Drum?

Did you know that drums can be more than just musical instruments? In some parts of Africa, drums can send messages! A talking drum can make different sounds. These sounds can be like words. People can understand these messages from far away. They use different rhythms and tones to communicate. It's like a secret language of drums! It's a very clever way to talk to people. This drum is not just a musical instrument; it's a way people talked to each other. It tells stories without words.

Coming Home to Ivory Coast!

Now, the good news! The country of France decided to send this special talking drum back. It is coming home to Ivory Coast! Everyone in Ivory Coast is very, very happy. They are so excited to have their drum back. It's like a lost friend finally returning home. This drum is a part of their history. It's a piece of their culture. It tells a story about who they are and where they come from.

Why is This So Important?

When the drum was taken, it was a sad thing. It was like losing a piece of their heart. It was a missing part of their story. Now that it is back, it makes everyone feel proud. It helps them remember their ancestors. It helps them remember their traditions. It's a symbol of their past and their future. This drum is very important for the people of Ivory Coast. It helps them connect to their roots and their identity.

More Treasures Coming Back

This is not just about one talking drum. Did you know that many other special things were also taken from Africa long ago? Things like old statues, masks, and other beautiful artworks. These are called 'cultural artefacts'. They are very important. This is just the beginning of a big, kind plan. This plan started in 2017. That's not too long ago!

A Big Plan for Africa

The country of France, and other countries too, are now working to return many of these special things. They want to send them back to the African countries they came from. They understand that these treasures belong to Africa. They belong to the people who made them. They belong to the countries where they were found. This is a very fair thing to do. It shows respect to Africa.

Did You Know?

Africa is a huge continent! It has 54 countries. Each country has its own special history and culture. Many of these countries have old treasures that tell their unique stories. Getting these treasures back helps preserve these stories. It helps children learn about their past. It makes everyone proud of their heritage. It connects new generations to their history.

A Happy Future

When special things like the talking drum come home, it helps everyone. It helps people in Ivory Coast feel stronger. It helps them feel connected to their past. It also helps other countries understand and respect Africa's rich history. It's a step towards being fairer to everyone. This makes the world a better place. It makes people happy and proud.

What Happens Next?

The talking drum will be kept in a special place in Ivory Coast. People will be able to see it. Children will be able to learn about it. It will remind everyone of their amazing history. It will be a symbol of pride for many, many years to come. This is truly wonderful news for Africa! Let's celebrate this happy return!
summary: France has returned a very special "talking drum" to Ivory Coast, bringing an important piece of history and culture back home to Africa after many years.
whyMatters: This is important because it means countries are giving back special things that were taken long ago, helping African countries get back their history and feel proud of their culture.

title: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
content: Partey is to appear at crown court next month, charged with raping a woman in December 2020.

originalTitle: Thousands in Madrid protest ‘forgotten’ Gaza, warn Iran war may spiral into
title: Friends Around the World Want Peace!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, bringing you news from our wonderful continent of Africa. Today, we're going to talk about something happening far, far away, but it's important because it's about people hoping for peace. Peace means everyone is safe and happy. We love to learn about what's happening in our world, even in places that are not in Africa. It helps us understand that we are all connected, like a big global family. Everyone, everywhere, wants to live in peace.

Lots of Friends Gathered Far Away

Did you know there's a big, beautiful city called Madrid? It's the capital city of a country called Spain, very far from Africa. Last weekend, lots and lots of people gathered together in Madrid. Imagine a whole playground full of children, but even more people! They came together because they wanted to share an important message. They didn't come to play games, but to stand side by side. They held up signs and they used their voices. This is called a 'protest'. When people protest, it means they are trying to tell others something very important that they care about a lot. They want grown-ups to listen to their message.

Why Did They Gather?

These kind people in Madrid gathered because they want an end to a 'war'. A 'war' is when people fight in a very big and serious way. It makes people sad, and it can hurt families and homes. The people in Madrid want this big fighting to stop. They want families to be safe again. They want children to be able to play and learn without worry. They want peace to come back to a place far away called Gaza. They were saying, "Please, let the fighting stop!" Did you know? Even if we are far away, we can still hope for peace for everyone. Wishing good things for others is a kind thing to do.

What is Happening in Gaza?

Gaza is a place where many, many families live. It's not a country, but it's part of a region where people have been living for a very long time. Right now, it's not a safe place because of the fighting. Homes have been broken, and it's hard for children to go to school or play outside. Imagine if your playground suddenly wasn't safe. That's how it feels for children in Gaza. The people in Madrid, and many other people around the world, are worried about the children and families in Gaza. They want everyone there to be safe and have food and a place to sleep.

Countries Far Away

The people protesting in Madrid were worried about fighting that involves a few different countries. Let's learn about them simply: * **Israel:** This is a country close to Gaza. Families live there too, and they also want to be safe from fighting. * **United States:** This is a very big country, super far away from Africa and from Gaza. It's a powerful country that sometimes helps other countries, and sometimes gets involved when there's trouble. * **Iran:** This is another country, also far away from Gaza. It's an important country in its part of the world. The people in Madrid were worried that the fighting might get bigger. Like a small argument that grows and grows, they don't want the fighting between these countries to get worse and involve even more places and people. They hope that the grown-ups in charge of these countries can find a way to stop the fighting and make peace. Did you know? The world has almost 200 different countries! Each one has its own people, languages, and stories. It's amazing how big our world is!

What Does "Protest" Mean?

When people protest, they are using their voices to make a change. They gather peacefully to show how they feel about something. They might hold signs, sing songs, or just stand together. It's a way for lots of people to say, "This is important to us!" It's like when you tell your parents what you really, really want, but imagine hundreds or thousands of people saying it all at once to the leaders of countries. The people in Madrid were protesting because they really, really want peace for Gaza and for all the countries involved.

Hoping for Peace, Everywhere

These people in Madrid reminded us that many grown-ups care about children and families everywhere in the world. They want to see an end to fighting, and they want to see everyone live in peace and safety. It's a reminder that even if a problem is far away, we can still think about it and hope for good things to happen. Just like we care about our friends and family here in Africa, we can also care about people we don't know, in places we haven't visited yet. Did you know? Peace is not just about no fighting. It's also about being kind to each other, sharing, and helping everyone feel safe and happy.

We All Want to Be Safe

So, my wonderful friends, the big news from far away Madrid is that many people are asking for peace. They want the fighting to stop in Gaza, and they hope that the countries involved can find a way to be friends again, or at least to stop fighting. It shows us that many people in the world want the same thing: a safe, happy, and peaceful place for all children and all families to live. Let's keep hoping for peace together! Goodbye for now from your African news reporter!
summary: Thousands of people in a city called Madrid, far away, gathered peacefully to ask for an end to the fighting and to hope for peace for children and families in a place called Gaza, and to wish that the fighting between big countries like the United States, Israel, and Iran does not get bigger.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that many people care about children and families who are not safe in faraway places. It reminds us that people all over the world wish for peace and want grown-ups to stop fighting, so everyone can live happily and safely.
